your_book:
Jaleel's book sits at $19,943, down 2.91% ($596.87) unrealized, and today was a split tape. The green came from the megacap-growth names: Adobe (16.2% weight) added 1.83%, ServiceNow (14%) gained 1.66%, and the headline mover was Rocket Lab, which jumped 15.93% — though it remains an AVOID at a 37.2 QARP and still sits down 7.1%. That rally is sentiment, not a thesis upgrade. Offsetting it, the defensive medical cluster sold off: Zoetis dropped 3.73%, Lululemon 2.79%, Danaher 1.74%, Boston Scientific and Abbott both around 1.5%.

Where price and thesis diverge: Adobe carries the top weight yet is the book's biggest loser at −15.8%, driven by AI-disruption narrative (Claude Design, CFO exit) despite a beat-and-raise — the model still calls it STRONG BUY at 77. Zoetis (84.6 QARP, STRONG BUY) and Boston Scientific (83 QARP) both rate top-tier on quality but show real losses (−7.0%, −9.2%), and BSX is wrestling a slashed FY26 guide. Lululemon is the cleanest red flag: −10.6%, a guidance cut, fifth straight negative Americas comp.

Watch BSX into its ~Jul 23 print and LULU's deteriorating fundamentals against a still-BUY rating.

attention.headline: Guidance cut, fundamentals weakening
attention.note: FY guide cut, fifth straight negative Americas comp and tariff-hit margins, position down 10.6% — thesis is eroding.

attention.headline: FY26 growth slashed, flat through Q3
attention.note: Guidance cut from 10.5% to 7-8.5% with soft Watchman/EP/urology, down 9.2% ahead of ~Jul 23 earnings.

attention.headline: 15.93% spike on an AVOID name
attention.note: Big one-day move but QARP of 37.2 and AVOID verdict mean this is sentiment, not an improving thesis.

attention.headline: Top weight, biggest loser
attention.note: 16.2% of the book and down 15.8% on AI-moat narrative despite a beat-and-raise and STRONG BUY rating.
